title: NOC 73111 – Glaziers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73111 professionals are the specialized tradespeople responsible for the cutting, fitting, and installation of glass in Canada's residential, commercial, and industrial buildings. As a TEER 3 occupation, Glaziers (also known as Architectural Glass and Metal Technicians) manage everything from massive glass curtain walls on skyscrapers to intricate stained glass in cultural centers. In 2026, as Canada mandates ultra-high-efficiency glazing for all new federal builds, these workers are classified as Green Tech Trade Talent. For immigration, this role is a prime candidate for the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws and the Ontario Skilled Trades Stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group731 – General construction tradesMinor Group7311 – Roofers, glaziers, painters, decorators and floor covering installersNOC Code73111

duties: Blueprint Interpretation: Read and interpret blueprints and specifications to determine glass type, thickness, frame requirements, and precise installation procedures.
Precision Cutting: Measure, mark, and cut glass using manual glass cutters or computerized cutting machines to exact tolerances.
Patterning & Tinting: Tint glass and create intricate patterns on glass surfaces by etching, sandblasting, or painting designs.
Scaffolding & Rigging: Assemble, erect, and dismantle scaffolds, rigging, and hoisting equipment to ensure safe access for high-rise or difficult installations.
Manual Positioning: Position large glass panes into frames and secure them using specialized clips, points, or architectural mouldings.
Prefabricated Installation: Assemble and install prefabricated glass units, mirrors, and various glass products on walls, ceilings, and building exteriors.
Metal Fabrication: Fabricate and attach aluminum or metal frames for glass installation in commercial storefronts and curtain wall systems.
Architectural Fitting: Install, fit, and attach architectural metals or related substitute products in both commercial and residential high-performance buildings.
Specialized Glass Work: Prepare and install skylights, showcases, aquariums, and stained glass in religious, cultural, or sports establishments.
Maintenance & Service: Repair and service residential windows and commercial aluminum doors; replace damaged glass or faulty sealants to maintain building envelope integrity.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three-to-four-year apprenticeship program is the standard path to qualification.
Experience: Alternatively, a combination of over four years of work experience and some industry courses in glazing may be accepted for trade certification.
Trade Certification:Compulsory in Quebec (as Erector Mechanic - Glazier).Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.
Compulsory in Quebec (as Erector Mechanic - Glazier).
Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended; it provides interprovincial mobility and is a massive asset in Federal Skilled Trades immigration applications.
Safety: In 2026, many employers require specific Fall Protection and Aerial Platform Operator certifications.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced Journeyman Glaziers or those specialized in structural curtain walls in cities like Vancouver and Toronto often earn annual salaries between $75,000 and $105,000.
job_outlook: The outlook is Balanced. Projections indicate that the sector will face moderate retirement rates through 2033. While some areas show limited growth in traditional glazing, the massive shift toward energy-efficient retrofits (replacing old windows with triple-pane units) is creating a steady stream of "Renovation Demand" that ensures long-term job security for skilled technicians.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Glaziers in 2026 is Balanced to Strong, with regional variations. In Alberta and British Columbia, the outlook is "Moderate" to "Good" due to significant commercial development and infrastructure projects. In Ontario, while the general forecast is "Limited" for some regions, major urban centers like Toronto have recently reported labor shortages for glaziers specialized in curtain wall installation. According to 2026 data, over 91% of glaziers work full-time, and approximately 93% of workers in this NOC receive at least one type of non-wage benefit, indicating a stable and competitive employment landscape.
Precision Engineering: Work with advanced "Smart Glass" (electrochromic glass), vacuum-insulated glazing, and complex architectural metal systems.
Diverse Work Environments: From high-rise construction sites using specialized crane rigging to high-end interior design and glass fabrication shops.
Premium Benefits: Glaziers in Canada enjoy one of the highest rates of medical, dental, and pension coverage in the construction trades.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC is frequently included in Category-Based Selection for Express Entry, offering a faster path to PR for those with trade experience.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): This NOC is a frequent target for category-based draws in 2026.
OINP Skilled Trades Stream: A dedicated route for glaziers with at least one year of experience in Ontario.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For those with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Skilled Trades BC: Streamlined pathway for certified glaziers to gain a provincial nomination in British Columbia.
